status_reason: Both API models ran. GPT-5.4 Pro (reasoning: high, 1245s, 3 hypotheses). Gemini 3.1 Pro (thinking: HIGH, 45s, 3 hypotheses). Consensus report written.

H1-009-C1.gemini_depth: Structural analogy (Michaelis-Menten NPQ-xanthophyll kinetic framework is formally transferable; DDE coupling function f([MEL]) undemonstrated -- structural gap prevents formal identity)
H1-009-C1.combined_recommendation: LOW PRIORITY as framed -- but single decisive experiment has HIGH feasibility. Reframe as: does exogenous melatonin shift Dt/(Dd+Dt) under 32C heat stress? Run PAM + HPLC pigment assay with melatonin +/- DTT before any mechanistic claim.

H2-009-C1.gemini_depth: Surface analogy (cascade provides additive stoichiometric capacity, NOT multiplicative kinetic flux amplification; C_eff formula is mathematically malformed -- confuses stoichiometry with rate)
H2-009-C1.combined_recommendation: NEEDS REWORK. Both models independently identified same mathematical error: stoichiometry vs kinetics conflation. Reframe to chronic/slow-ROS context. First experiment: isotope-traced [13C]-melatonin LC-MS/MS to confirm AFMK/AMK actually form in heat-stressed Symbiodiniaceae before any functional claim.

H6-009-C1.gemini_depth: Formal isomorphism (d[MEL]/dt = J_syn * H_dark - k_deg * ROS_base * Q10^(dT/10) * [MEL]; predicts 19% pre-dawn melatonin depletion per +3C nighttime warming; Durusdinium must have ~1.23x higher SNAT flux than Cladocopium to explain 3C tolerance gap)
H6-009-C1.combined_recommendation: PROMISING for targeted resolution. Widest divergence in session. Gemini validated formal kinetic structure (9/10); GPT challenged biological premises (Antolin 1997 shows stress increases melatonin, not depletion; dark ROS too low). Resolve with split-night temperature experiment: 30/26 vs 26/30 day/night at matched mean heat, pre-dawn melatonin metabolomics, pre-dawn add-back rescue. If pre-dawn [MEL] falls under warm nights, GPT challenge is addressed. Do not start with transcript mining -- use enzyme activity assays.

key_divergences: H6-009-C1: widest gap in session -- Gemini 9/10 formal isomorphism (kinetic ODE structure valid; 19% depletion prediction; 1.23x SNAT ratio) vs GPT 3/10 CHALLENGE (Antolin 1997 shows melatonin INCREASES under stress, not depletes; dark cells have low ROS; SNAT transcript is weak biomarker layer in post-transcriptionally regulated dinoflagellates)

H1-009-C1: GPT CHALLENGE vs Gemini NEUTRAL (both at 4/10; GPT emphasizes zero empirical basis for melatonin-DDE activation; Gemini notes structural analogy is formally sound but coupling function f([MEL]) unmeasured)
key_agreements: H2-009-C1: both models independently identified the same mathematical flaw -- the cascade provides stoichiometric (additive) capacity, not kinetic (multiplicative) amplification. Rate-limiting step is still v_1 = k_1[MEL][ROS] regardless of downstream metabolites.

H1-009-C1: both confirm melatonin-NPQ link at 26C (Roopin 2013) is real and grounded; both flag DDE activation by melatonin as undemonstrated; both propose DTT-blocking experiment as decisive mechanistic test.

All three hypotheses: both models flag dinoflagellate post-transcriptional dominance as a weakness of transcript-level predictions; both recommend proteome/metabolome over transcriptome for PRJNA723630 analysis.
All three hypotheses: TPH-first pathway assumption not confirmed in Symbiodiniaceae; HMM-based pathway-agnostic search recommended over KEGG/AANAT annotation.
shared_technical_corrections: Pathway assumption: canonical animal TPH-first pathway not confirmed in Symbiodiniaceae -- use pathway-agnostic HMM search for both TDC-first and TPH-first enzyme families
Post-transcriptional dominance: transcript levels in Camp et al. 2022 are weak proxy for enzyme activity; proteome and metabolome layers are more informative
Concentration-response: any assay should include 10 nM to 100 uM range to test whether endogenous nM concentrations are sufficient vs pharmacological uM effects
1O2 vs OH: the dominant ROS in heat-stressed chloroplasts is singlet oxygen, not hydroxyl radical -- quantitative models must use k(MEL + 1O2) ~ 5e7 not k(MEL + OH) ~ 2.7e10
